steps.title: Identify what the blank must mean
steps.content: The sentence reads: “______ of this approach cite the production of less material waste and a faster return on investment.” The blank is the subject and must name a group of people who actively advocate for modular construction and would cite its benefits.

steps.title: Eliminate each choice
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘Components’: Components are the physical parts or elements of a system, not people. Parts of a building cannot “cite” benefits of a construction method. ✗
  • Choice B — ‘Safeguards’: Safeguards are protective measures or precautions. They are not a category of people, so they cannot cite advantages of an approach. ✗
  • Choice C — ‘Proponents’: Proponents are people who advocate for or support something. Proponents of modular construction would naturally cite its benefits — less waste, faster ROI — to make their case. ✓
  • Choice D — ‘Epitomes’: Epitomes are perfect examples or embodiments of something. An epitome of an approach is not a person who argues for it, so it cannot “cite” evidence. ✗
That confirms C is correct.
